name: Regional Missouri Bank
description_short: Regional Missouri Bank is a community bank serving Brookfield, Moberly, and Salisbury, MO with FDIC-insured checking, savings, loans, and business banking products.
description_long:
Regional Missouri Bank is a traditional community bank operating in Missouri with physical locations in Brookfield, Moberly, Salisbury, and Marceline. The bank is FDIC-insured and positions itself as a local financial institution serving personal and business customers throughout the region.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of retail banking products including multiple checking and savings account tiers (Kasasa Cash checking, regular savings, money market accounts, CDs, and IRAs), personal loans, auto loans, and mortgage lending. For business customers, they provide business checking, business credit cards, and specialized lending products including agriculture loans, equipment loans, livestock loans, and farm & land loans. They also offer personal credit cards and services like bill pay, eStatements, and electronic banking tools.

Regional Missouri Bank distinguishes itself through a strong focus on agricultural lending and community-based service, with dedicated agriculture loan products and messaging emphasizing local relationships. They offer surcharge-free ATM access through the MoneyPass network, mobile banking, debit card controls, and financial education resources. The bank has undergone a merger with BNC and markets itself around the theme of local decision-making and community roots.

As a regional community bank, Regional Missouri Bank is best suited for customers prioritizing local relationships and community banking over digital-first experiences. The website demonstrates full-service banking capabilities but does not highlight competitive advantages in rates, technology innovation, or specialized lending beyond agriculture. Customers should compare rates and fees against larger regional and national competitors before opening accounts.
services: Free Kasasa Cash Checking accounts with rewards
Personal savings accounts and money market accounts
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) and Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs)
Home mortgage loans, construction loans, and land loans
Business checking and business money market accounts
Agriculture loans including livestock loans, farm & land loans, equipment loans, and operating lines of credit
Business credit cards and personal credit cards
Surcharge-free ATM network access through MoneyPass
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Surcharge-free ATM access nationwide through MoneyPass network
Specialized agriculture lending including livestock loans, farm & land loans, and operating lines of credit
Multiple checking account options including free Kasasa Cash and Kasasa Cash Back accounts
Mobile banking app available on iOS and Android for 24/7 account access
Debit card controls app allowing budget limits and card restrictions
Comprehensive personal and business loan products including mortgages, auto loans, and construction loans
cons: Limited to three primary physical locations (Brookfield, Moberly, Salisbury) with limited geographic reach
Website does not display current rates, APYs, or fee schedules publicly
No mention of online account opening process; apply/enroll buttons present but actual requirements unclear
No indication of whether accounts can be opened remotely or require in-person visits
Limited information about competitive advantages in rates, credit standards, or loan approval processes
best_for: Agricultural businesses and farmers needing specialized farm loans, equipment financing, and livestock lending
Residents of Brookfield, Moberly, Salisbury, and Marceline seeking relationship-based local banking
Small business owners requiring comprehensive business checking and lending products
Customers prioritizing FDIC-insured community banking over digital-only neobanks
diagnosis: Regional Missouri Bank is best for agricultural businesses, small business owners, and community-focused customers in central Missouri seeking personalized local banking relationships with FDIC insurance. The main caveat is geographic limitation to three primary locations and lack of publicly available rate/fee information, requiring direct contact for accurate product pricing comparison.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
